title: Handle request for Google login under Internet Identity-only auth constraints

requirements.text: Update all sign-in UI to clearly state that authentication is provided via Internet Identity and that Google OAuth login is not available in this app, using concise English copy in the existing sign-in surfaces (navbar auth button and the RequireAuth sign-in card).
acceptanceCriteria: In the navbar sign-in button (frontend/src/components/AuthButton.tsx), the unauthenticated label references Internet Identity (e.g., "Sign In (Internet Identity)") or otherwise makes it explicit that the sign-in method is Internet Identity.

In the gated-access sign-in card (frontend/src/routes/RequireAuth.tsx), the descriptive text explicitly says the user will sign in with Internet Identity and does not mention Google login as an available option.
All user-facing text added/changed is in English and consistent across the app.

requirements.text: Add a short FAQ entry to the Help page explaining that Google login is not supported and that users should use Internet Identity instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Help page (frontend/src/pages/HelpPage.tsx) includes a new FAQ accordion item stating Google login is not available and Internet Identity is the supported sign-in method.
The new FAQ entry is written in clear English and does not instruct users to use any unsupported third-party auth provider.

constraints: Do not implement Google OAuth / Google Sign-In (third-party authentication) because this app stack only supports Internet Identity for authentication.

Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui (immutable paths).
Backend remains single-actor Motoko; no additional backend services or external auth providers may be introduced.
nonGoals: Implementing Google OAuth flows (authorization code exchange, token verification, refresh tokens).
Linking Google accounts to Internet Identity anchors or adding any other third-party identity provider integration.
